subject: LU-9066 ldlm: don't evict client on umount if AST fails

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

the time of forming the blast list is too early, anything may change by the time the RPC will be sent. it should be done right there, at the time of RPC creation or even sending. I would adjust ldlm_handle_ast_error to avoid eviction if the import is closed, this way all the ASTs will be covered at once. we could also consider returning some special code from ptlrpc_import_delay_req and hide this new logic right there.

Patch Set 1:

Vitaly, I think nothing can change an export sate back from 'disconnected' or 'fail', isn't it? So doing this while forming ast list looks ok to me.

Patch Set 1:

anything can still change it forth from full to disconnected in between

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Vitaly, I've experienced some problems when this happens, so it is not just a message but after it something goes wrong. I see two problems here:
1) AST work processing is aborted in the middle when such error happens, so if there is several items in the list then rest of it will not be processed though they may belong to other healthy export. Now this case returns -ERESTART so it can proceed just skipping outdated lock.
2) for lock from disconnected export the class_fail_export() will be called, so export will became failed in the middle of disconnection process. This can be the reason for LU-8359 for example.

So this is more cleanup/fix patch I'd say. Correct me if I am mistaken.

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

>  if there is several items in the list then rest of it will not be processed though they may belong to other healthy export

ah, this is worth to be added to the commit message.

commit.subject: LU-9066 ldlm: don't evict client on umount if AST fails
commit.message:
LU-9066 ldlm: don't evict client on umount if AST fails

When server is being stopped the AST send is denied and
that AST failure will lead to the unexpected client
eviction.
Patch adds extra checks in ldlm_handle_ast_error() to
distinguish that type of error and proceed without
eviction.
